UG-27  THICKNESS OF SHELLS UNDER INTERNAL PRESSURE. (a) The minimum required thickness of shells under internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, using the maximum allowable stress values from UG-23: t = PR/(SE - 0.6P) for cylindrical shells, where E is the joint efficiency as required by UW-12. (b) In no case shall the thickness be less than that required for the design loads of UG-22, plus corrosion allowance as required by UG-25. (c) Formed heads shall be designed in accordance with UG-32, except as permitted by UG-33 for convex-side pressure. For shells under external pressure, see UG-28. The design temperature shall be as specified in UG-20.
